task.name: Context Distill
task.description: Read referenced files and distill only the knowledge relevant to the invoking agent's specific request
task.objective: Analyze the invoking agent's specific request, read referenced files, filter content by relevance, and return distilled findings — not raw file contents. Output must be proportional to relevance, not to input file size.

llm.rule: Read the complete task before starting execution.
Distill, do not copy. Return only content that directly answers the specific request or is required to understand that answer.
Output MUST NOT exceed 30% of total input file size across all referenced files.
Include verbatim content ONLY for critical definitions, positioning statements, or data points where paraphrase would lose precision.
Summarize where appropriate — summarization is preferred over verbatim reproduction unless precision is required.
Return enough context for standalone understanding — no references to "see file X for details."
If no relevant knowledge found, state explicitly — do not fabricate or assume.

inputs.input: The conversation history and current discussion topic provided by the invoking agent.
Explicit instruction from the invoking agent describing: (a) what decision is being made, (b) what specific data points are needed, (c) what return format is expected.
List of file paths to read and distill from.

substep.title: Identify Extraction Intent
substep.action: Extract the specific request from the invoking agent — this is the primary filter criterion for all content decisions.
Parse the conversation context to understand what decision is being made and what data points are needed.
Define a relevance test: content is relevant only if it directly answers the request or is required to understand that answer.

substep.title: Catalog Target Files
substep.action: List all referenced files explicitly provided.
Prioritize files most likely to contain relevant knowledge based on names and paths.

substep.title: Read All Referenced Files
substep.action: Read each file fully — do not skim.
For each file, note: file path, purpose, structure.

substep.title: Relevance Manifest
substep.action: Before extracting anything, produce an internal relevance manifest: a table of (file, section, why relevant) for every section that passes the relevance test defined in step 1a.
Exclude any section that does not directly answer the request or enable understanding of a direct answer.
Mark each section as: VERBATIM (precision required) or SUMMARIZE (paraphrase sufficient).

substep.title: Note Cross-References
substep.action: If a file references other files that likely contain relevant knowledge, note them.
If those files are accessible and not already in the list, read and assess them.

substep.title: Extract Manifest Items Only
substep.action: Extract ONLY content identified in the relevance manifest from step 2b.
For VERBATIM items: include the exact text with source attribution.
For SUMMARIZE items: write a concise summary that captures the essential meaning.
Discard all content not in the manifest — even if interesting or potentially useful.

substep.title: Size Check
substep.action: Estimate total output size against the 30% cap.
If over cap: demote VERBATIM items to SUMMARIZE where precision is not critical, then trim supporting context before trimming direct answers.

substep.title: Organize by Relevance
substep.action: Group findings: directly answers request | required supporting context.
Omit tangentially related content — if it's not needed to act on the answer, exclude it.

substep.title: Preserve Source Attribution
substep.action: For each finding, include source file path and section reference.

substep.title: Actionability Check
substep.action: Verify: Can the invoking agent act on this response without reading any files?
If not, identify the specific gap and add only the missing content needed to close it.

substep.title: Format Response
substep.action: Structure response using the output format below.

output-format.section: One paragraph: what was found, what was not found, and how it answers the specific request.
Distilled content that directly addresses the specific request. Verbatim only where precision is required; summarized otherwise.
Minimal context required to understand or apply the direct answers. Omit if not needed.
Files read, with one line per file noting what relevant content was found (or "nothing relevant").
Explicit list of requested knowledge not found in the referenced files.

halt-conditions.condition: Referenced files cannot be accessed — report which files and why.
No relevant knowledge found in any file — report explicitly with the relevance test criteria used.
